docs.title: Welcome to Composable
docs.text: Composable is a powerful CLI and registry of Docker Compose fragments designed to streamline your development workflow.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• 🚀 Instant Service Provisioning: Add databases, brokers, and AI models to your project with a single command.
  • 🧱 Modular Architecture: Use the extends syntax to keep your main compose.yml clean and maintainable.
  • 📦 Pre-configured Stacks: Initialize entire application environments (like Django + Postgres + Celery) in seconds.
  • 🔄 Smart Build & Watch: Automatically inject build and watch configurations into your project.
  • 🌍 Global Configuration: Set global defaults for your preferred development environment.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: npm install -g @voidrot/composable

docs.title: Adding a Service
docs.text: composable add compose postgresql

docs.title: Initializing a Stack
docs.text: composable stack init django-base

docs.title: Documentation Roadmap
docs.text:
• Commands Reference
  • Fragments Registry
  • Stacks Guide
  • Global Configuration

docs.title: add <type> <name>
docs.text:
Adds a fragment to the current project.

  • Type: The category of the fragment (e.g., compose).
  • Name: The name of the fragment (e.g., postgresql).
  • Options:
  • -e, --extend: Add the service directly to an existing compose.yml using extends syntax.
  • -n, --name <customName>: Override the name of the service.
  • --no-build: Do not inject build configurations from the fragment into the compose.yml.
  • --no-watch: Do not inject develop.watch configurations from the fragment into the compose.yml.

docs.title: stack init <name>
docs.text:
Initializes a stack by adding all of its configured fragments.

  • Name: The name of the stack (e.g., django-base).
  • Options:
  • -e, --extend: Extend an existing compose file (enabled by default).
  • --no-build: Do not inject build configurations from the stack's fragments.
  • --no-watch: Do not inject develop.watch configurations from the stack's fragments.

docs.title: search [query]
docs.text: Search for available fragments in the registry.

docs.title: cache --all
docs.text: Download all available fragments to the local cache ($HOME/.composable/fragments).

docs.title: update
docs.text: Update the local cache with the latest fragments from the registry.

docs.title: init-metadata <path>
docs.text: Developer Tool: Scans a .yml file for ${VAR} syntax and generates a corresponding .json metadata file.

docs.title: Global Configuration {#config}
docs.text: Composable can be configured globally via ~/.composable/config.yml.

docs.title: Example Configuration
docs.text:
registries:
  - name: default
    url: https://voidrot.github.io/composable/latest
defaults:
  env_file: true
  build: true
  watch: true

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
• registries: A list of fragment registries to use.
  • defaults: Global defaults for CLI options:
    • env_file: Whether to add env_file: - .env.compose by default.
    • build: Whether to inject build configurations by default.
    • watch: Whether to inject develop.watch configurations by default.

docs.title: Release and Versioning
docs.text:
This project uses Release Please with Conventional Commits:

  • fix: produces a patch release.
  • feat: produces a minor release.
  • feat!: (or any type!:) produces a major release.

docs.title: Getting Started with Composable
docs.text: Composable is a collection of Docker Compose fragments and a CLI tool to manage them.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install the CLI from npm:

npm install -g @voidrot/composable

You can also run without global install:

npx @voidrot/composable search

For local development, clone the repository and build from source:

git clone https://github.com/voidrot/composable.git
cd composable
npm install
npm run build
npm link

docs.title: Adding your first fragment
docs.text:
To add a PostgreSQL fragment to your project:

composable add compose postgresql

This will:

  1. Fetch the postgresql.yml fragment.
  2. Store it in ./.compose/postgresql.yml.
  3. Update your local .env file with default environment variables.

docs.title: Fragments
docs.text: Fragments are the building blocks of Composable. Each fragment consists of a .yml template and a .json metadata file.

docs.title: JSON Schema
docs.text:
To ensure fragments are valid, a JSON schema is available at schemas/fragment.schema.json. You can reference it in your fragment JSON files to enable IDE validation:

{
  "$schema": "../../schemas/fragment.schema.json",
  "name": "my-fragment"
}

The schema defines standard fields such as name, description, variables, and configs. It also supports optional build and watch objects which can be automatically injected into your project's top-level Docker Compose file.

docs.title: Available Compose Fragments
docs.text:
• Databases & Caching:
  • postgresql
  • redis
  • valkey
  • chromadb
  • arcadedb
  • Message Brokers:
  • rabbitmq
  • nats
  • eclipse-mosquitto
  • AI / ML:
  • ollama
  • vllm
  • Web & Routing:
  • traefik
  • caddy
  • Observability:
  • grafana
  • influxdb
  • jaeger
  • otel-collector
  • Application Frameworks:
  • django
  • celery
  • celery-beat
  • celery-flower

docs.title: Stacks
docs.text: Stacks represent collections of fragments designed to work together as a cohesive architecture. Stacks are defined using JSON configuration files.

docs.title: JSON Schema
docs.text:
To ensure your stacks are valid, a JSON schema is available at schemas/stack.schema.json. You can reference it in your stack JSON files:

{
  "$schema": "../schemas/stack.schema.json",
  "name": "my-stack",
  "fragments": []
}

    • celery-flower
